taskDescription: Complete the crossword on the posterior abdominal wall (AN45.1-3).

words.clue: Multilayered fascia enclosing the back extensor muscles, especially developed in the lumbar region
Major hip flexor muscle forming the lateral part of the posterior abdominal wall, arising from lumbar vertebrae
Largest branch of the lumbar plexus (L2-L4) supplying quadriceps and anterior thigh skin
Nerve from L2-L4 supplying the adductor compartment of the medial thigh
Condition of lateral femoral cutaneous nerve compression causing anterolateral thigh paraesthesia
Group of back muscles (iliocostalis, longissimus, spinalis) responsible for vertebral extension
Quadrilateral muscle forming the posterior abdominal wall lateral to psoas, attached to 12th rib
Tuberculous disease of the lumbar vertebrae causing abscess tracking along the psoas sheath
